mission.title: caddy: Metrics monitoring in v2
mission.description: Metrics monitoring in v2. This issue affects 14+ users.

steps.title: Identify caddy feature symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your caddy deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cert-manager -l app=caddy
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=caddy -n cert-manager --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors related to: Metrics monitoring in v2

steps.title: Check current resource configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant caddy resources:
```bash
kubectl get resource -A
kubectl describe resource <name> -n <namespace>
```
I've been using the Prometheus support in Caddy 1.x. This has worked extremely well. I would like to be able to upgrade to v2, but it doesn't seem like there's any out of the box monitoring options.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Metrics monitoring in v2
Confirm Metrics monitoring in v2 is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=caddy -n cert-manager --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n cert-manager --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
The root cause is: @SuperQ

> Do you want to take a first pass

Sure - I can try and fit this in over the next couple days.

> If the metrics handler doubles as a Caddy HTTP handler module like I described, then the listening port and path are all taken care of by the user's config, no need to separately configure that in the metrics module.

@mholt If I'm interpreting this right, I think what you're envisioning is . This fixes the issue because it addresses the underlying problem that was causing the failure.

prerequisites.description: A working caddy installation or development environment.
